title: Gomesa cornigera
taxonomy: Gomesa cornigera is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It is a type of sympodial epiphyte, meaning it grows by sending out new stems from its base. The classification of Gomesa cornigera is also often disputed, with some sources considering it a synonym of the genus Nidifica. However, the most widely accepted classification is as a member of the genus Gomesa within the subtribe Oncidiinae. Further research may be needed to fully understand its classification and relationships within the orchid family.

The plant is said to possess anti-inflammatory properties and is used to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
